name: openalex-database
description: Query and analyze scholarly literature using the OpenAlex database. This skill should be used when searching for academic papers, analyzing research trends, finding works by authors or institutions, tracking citations, discovering open access publications, or conducting bibliometric analysis across 240M+ scholarly works. Use for literature searches, research output analysis, citation analysis, and academic database queries.
capabilities: Search 240M+ scholarly works by title, abstract, or topic
Find papers by author or institution with full publication history
Discover highly cited recent papers and open access publications
Analyze publication trends over time and research output by entity
Batch lookup multiple DOIs, ORCIDs, or other identifiers efficiently
Extract large datasets with pagination, sampling, and CSV export
author: davila7
securitySummary: This is a legitimate scholarly research tool for querying the OpenAlex academic database. All static findings are false positives: markdown backticks were flagged as shell commands, URL parameters and markdown tables were flagged as cryptographic algorithms, and documentation links were flagged as reconnaissance. The Python code makes HTTP requests to the public OpenAlex API for legitimate academic research purposes.
valueStatement: Research requires finding relevant academic papers and analyzing publication trends. This skill provides direct access to the OpenAlex database containing 240M+ scholarly works, enabling literature searches, citation analysis, and research output evaluation without manual database navigation.
userTitle: Query scholarly literature from OpenAlex database
limitations: No API key required but polite pool with email provides 10x rate limit boost
Rate limited to 1 request per second by default (10 with email)
Only searches OpenAlex database; does not query PubMed, Scopus, or Web of Science
Analysis functions are read-only; cannot modify or contribute to the database

useCases.title: Literature review automation
useCases.description: Find and organize relevant papers for literature reviews by searching topics, filtering by year and citations.

useCases.title: Research output assessment
useCases.description: Analyze publication trends and research impact for institutions, departments, or individual researchers.

useCases.title: Citation discovery and tracking
useCases.description: Find papers that cite specific works and discover related research through citation networks.

promptTemplates.title: Find papers by topic
promptTemplates.scenario: Search for recent papers on a research topic
promptTemplates.prompt: Find 50 papers about {topic} published since {year}, sorted by citations. Show the title, year, and citation count for each.

promptTemplates.title: Analyze researcher output
promptTemplates.scenario: Get publication metrics for an author
promptTemplates.prompt: Analyze the research output of {researcher_name} from {institution}. Show total publications, open access percentage, and top research topics since {year}.

promptTemplates.title: Track research trends
promptTemplates.prompt: Show me publication trends for {field} over the last 10 years. How many papers were published each year and what is the growth rate?

promptTemplates.title: Open access discovery
promptTemplates.prompt: Find 100 open access papers on {topic} published in {year}. Exclude bronze OA. Export to CSV with DOI and title.

bestPractices: Always provide an email address to initialize the client for 10x rate limit boost
Use the two-step pattern for entity lookups: first get the ID, then filter by ID
Use batch operations and maximum page size (200) for efficient large-scale data extraction
antiPatterns: Do not filter by entity names directly; use the two-step pattern to get IDs first
Avoid sequential API calls for multiple IDs; use batch_lookup instead
Do not use random page numbers for sampling; use the sample parameter with seed

faq.question: Is an API key required to use OpenAlex?
faq.answer: No, OpenAlex is completely open and free. Providing an email gives access to the polite pool with 10x rate limits.

faq.question: How many scholarly works are in the database?
faq.answer: OpenAlex contains 240M+ scholarly works including articles, books, datasets, and preprints from around the world.

faq.question: What rate limits apply to API requests?
faq.answer: Default is 1 request per second. With an email address in the polite pool, you get 10 requests per second.

faq.question: Can I export data to CSV or other formats?
faq.answer: Yes, the skill includes examples for exporting results to CSV files using Python's csv module.

faq.question: Does the skill support finding papers that cite a specific work?
faq.answer: Yes, use the cited_by_api_url from a work entity to retrieve all papers that cite it.

faq.question: What external identifiers can I use for lookups?
faq.answer: You can use DOI for works, ORCID for authors, ROR for institutions, and ISSN for sources.
